title: Podman login tries to map uid of user that I use to login instead of build user
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug



**Description**

When running podman login -u myuser -p password myregistry I get the following error:

17:29:51  time="2022-06-10T17:29:51+02:00" level=warning msg="\"/\" is not a shared mount, this could cause issues or missing mounts with rootless containers"
17:29:51  time="2022-06-10T17:29:51+02:00" level=error msg="cannot find UID/GID for user mysuer: No subuid ranges found for user \"myuser\" in /etc/subuid - check rootless mode in man pages."
17:29:51  time="2022-06-10T17:29:51+02:00" level=warning msg="Using rootless single mapping into the namespace. This might break some images. Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid for adding sub*ids if not using a network user"
17:29:51  Login Succeeded!

I am running podman rootless on a Kubernetes pod. Podman is running with build user so I have the build user in subuid and subgid

cat /etc/subuid
  build:2000:50000
cat /etc/subgid
 build:2000:50000

But obviously I don't have myuser.

If I run the id command then it shows me build user

id
uid=1000(build) gid=1000(build) groups=1000(build),1000680000



**Describe the results you received:**


**Describe the results you expected:**


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Runnig podman build in Kubernetes agent in Jenkins. Some jobs work but other don't work. It is same pod template

**Output of `podman version`:**

No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: @GolanTrevize10 what happens if you specify another user (e.g., `-u foobar`)?
If I put `-u foobar` then it is trying also to map myuser instead of foobar. Don't understand why. Maybe there is some podman configuration file somewhere

Thanks for checking, @GolanTrevize10.  So it's not related to the CLI option.

@rhatdan @giuseppe PTAL

Could you try with a newer version of POdman within the container?  Myuser should not be looked up in /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid.  As a matter of fact podman login should not be entering the user namespace at all.
Strange thing, if I run `podman info` before `podman login` then it works. I am running podman on ubi8 container and I think the newer version is not available as a package for ubi yet.
So this looks like podman info created the user namespace, and then podman login can just join it.

And what can be the reason for that? I tried with podman 4.0.2 and same
issue

El lun., 13 jun. 2022 19:37, Daniel J Walsh ***@***.***>

```
+ id
09:29:06  uid=1000(build) gid=1000(build) groups=1000(build),1000680000
```

Thanks.  Is `HOME` pointing to the right directory?

Also, how have you created the container?  Any volume?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we never heard back, I am assuming this is fixed and closing.
